lesson.menu_desc: Negotiating and building partnerships: proposals, terms and reaching agreement (first conditional + agreement language) -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Negotiating & Building Partnerships (first conditional / agreement language)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 17 -- Negotiating & Building Partnerships

listenings.text: Hi Marcos, this is Diana from Northbridge Valuation in Toronto. I am calling about our partnership proposal. We really like your firm, and we want to move forward. Here is our offer: if you join our international network this year, we will give you full access to our cross-border deals. If your team completes the joint training, we will share our valuation models too. We are flexible on the terms. If you need more time to review, we can meet in the middle and start with a small pilot project. We believe this is a fair deal for both sides, and we are ready to make a strong commitment. Please review the terms and call me back. If we agree this week, we will sign before the end of the month. I look forward to building something great together. Talk soon.

Let me summarize the agreement we reached today. After a long negotiation, both sides made a few concessions, and I am happy with the result. First, if we deliver the pilot project on time, Northbridge will open their full network to us next year. Second, we agreed on the financial terms: a fair split, with a small fee in the first year only. Third, if either side wants to leave, they will give ninety days notice. We did not get everything we wanted, but we found a good compromise. This partnership gives us real leverage in the international market. It is a strong, long-term commitment, and I believe it is a great deal for our firm. Tomorrow, I will send the final terms to the board for approval.
